extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) holds on every reachable execution. Proof by invariant: opted_out becomes true only via [t1], which simultaneously sets sent:=false, so the state immediately after optout satisfies opted_out=true AND sent=false. No transition ever resets opted_out back to false (only [t1] writes it, and only to true), so once opted_out is true it stays true. The only transition that sets sent:=true is [t2], whose guard is "not opted_out" — therefore [t2] is disabled whenever opted_out is true and can never re-assert sent in that region. The remaining transitions enabled while opted_out is true all keep sent false: [t3] (send_attempt under opted_out) sets sent:=false, [t4] (tick) sets sent:=false, and [t1] (optout) sets sent:=false. While opted_out is false the antecedent of the implication is false, so the property is vacuously satisfied regardless of sent (e.g., after a send_attempt setting sent=true). Thus in every reachable state, opted_out=true implies sent=false, for any event sequence of any length (including the bounded 10-event check). No counterexample exists.
